narration: Your company matches your 401k — sounds like free money, right? Here's what they don't tell you: that match might not actually be yours yet.
visual: Amber piggy bank SVG springs in with a black padlock snapping shut on top; text overlay fades in: YOUR MATCH ISN'T YOURS YET; subtitle: THE VESTING TRAP

narration: It's called vesting. Most plans make you wait three full years before the company's money is legally yours. Leave before year three and you walk away with zero.
visual: Horizontal timeline of Year 1 through Year 6 with lock icons; years 1 and 2 show gray locked padlocks labeled 0%; year 3 unlocks in green labeled 100%; warning box: QUIT BEFORE YEAR 3 = $0

narration: The average match is $3,500 a year. Two years of contributions sitting in your account — quit on year two and your employer legally takes back all $7,000.
visual: Three growing bar charts: Year 1 $3,500, Year 2 $7,000, Year 3 $10,500 in amber; bars for years 1 and 2 then drain to zero and turn red labeled FORFEITED; large text: -$7,000

narration: 57% of 401k plans have vesting rules. One in three workers quits before they're fully vested — most of them never even knew they left that money behind.
visual: Grid of 9 person silhouettes; 3 silhouettes turn gray with red X marks representing the one-in-three; stat cards animate in showing 57% and 1-IN-3

narration: Some companies use graded vesting — you earn 20% per year over five years. Still, quit at year two and you're only 40% vested, forfeiting $4,200 back to your boss.
visual: Five horizontal progress bars labeled Year 1-5, each filling to 20% 40% 60% 80% 100% with dollar amounts; red forfeit callout at bottom: QUIT YEAR 2 = LOSE $4,200

narration: Before you quit your next job, look up your vesting date. Waiting even a few extra months could mean keeping $14,000 that's already sitting in your account. Follow for more.
visual: Calendar SVG with a date circled and green checkmark animating in; text: CHECK BEFORE YOU QUIT; three action step cards slide in; amber CTA banner: FOLLOW FOR MORE MONEY TRAPS
